name: crash_only_as_committed_external_body
reason: `lemma_if_no_outstanding_writes_then_persistent_memory_view_can_only_crash_as_committed` is `external_body` — the uniqueness of crash states when no writes are outstanding is trusted without proof; if `can_crash_as` admitted extra states, crash recovery could observe non-committed data

name: can_crash_as_allows_committed
code:
proof fn phi_2_can_crash_as_allows_committed(view: PersistentMemoryRegionView)
    requires
        view.no_outstanding_writes(),
    ensures
        view.can_crash_as(view.committed()),
{
}
reason: The committed state should always be a valid crash state when there are no outstanding writes — if `can_crash_as` rejected committed, no valid crash state would exist

name: chunk_ignoring_and_after_flush_same_when_no_writes
code:
proof fn phi_3_chunk_ignoring_and_after_flush_same_when_no_writes(view: PersistentMemoryRegionView, chunk: int, bytes: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        view.no_outstanding_writes(),
    ensures
        view.chunk_corresponds_ignoring_outstanding_writes(chunk, bytes) <==> view.chunk_corresponds_after_flush(chunk, bytes),
{
}
reason: With no outstanding writes, `flush_byte()` equals `state_at_last_flush` for every byte — the two chunk predicates should be equivalent; if they diverged, the crash model would be inconsistent

name: regions_committed_length
reason: The committed sequence should have one entry per region — if `Seq::new` produced a different length, indexing into `committed()` would be misaligned with the regions

name: can_crash_as_empty_regions
reason: Empty regions should crash as the empty sequence — if the empty case failed, initialization of the persistent memory system couldn't establish crash consistency
